craftToolCompliance.T5_ToneContract: Maintained morally complex and atmospherically taut tone. Avoided melodrama through clinical assessment language. Dry wit in 'The file was wrong about many things' and Lirien's parenthetical observations.
craftToolCompliance.T6_StakesEscalation: Stakes elevated from personal (fulfill contract) to strategic (Warden-lineage revelation means execution may worsen Pallor crisis). Lirien now facing operational paradox.
craftToolCompliance.T7_BlackMoment: Groundwork laid — dramatic irony of Kaelith trusting someone contractually bound to kill her, with reader aware of both the contract and now the paradox that killing her may destroy god-seal access.
craftToolCompliance.T8_InternalExternalResistance: Internal: Lirien's operative conditioning vs emerging curiosity/respect for Kaelith. External: binding contract vs Seep directive to gather intelligence. 48-hour deadline introduced.
craftToolCompliance.T10_SandersonsLaws: Oathbrand mechanics clarified — suppresses but doesn't remove capacity, responds to ontological disturbances if subject has Warden inheritance. Pallor failure established as measurable, trackable phenomenon.
craftToolCompliance.T12_DualArcInterleave: Fantasy beat (Pallor tear + Oathbrand flare revealing Warden capacity) creates romantic complication (killing Kaelith becomes strategically catastrophic, not just morally complex).
craftToolCompliance.T13_TensionMapping: Tension level 5 maintained. Three escalating beats: surveillance assessment (operational), direct eye contact (interpersonal), intelligence packet (world-scale crisis introduction).
craftToolCompliance.T15_ForeshadowingPayoff: FS-003 advanced (surveillance becomes mutual recognition). FS-009 planted (Ythara reference as imprisoned god). FS-005 introduced (Warden-lineage paradox).
craftToolCompliance.T16_SymbolicMotif: Water/infiltration imagery ('current' metaphors in Lirien's thought patterns, 'seepage' of human language into fae precision). Oathbrand as wound that shouldn't respond but does.
craftToolCompliance.T17_VoiceFingerprinting: Lirien's voice distinct from Kaelith: longer sentences with observation→assessment structure, operative vocabulary ('variables,' 'parameters,' 'calculate'), fae precision markers ('I have observed,' avoids contractions), clinical emotional register.
craftToolCompliance.T18_InformationAsymmetry: Reader knows kill contract (ch1), Kaelith suspects threat but doesn't know specifics. Lirien learns about Warden inheritance (reader and Lirien both learn together). Dramatic irony maintained.
craftToolCompliance.T20_EmotionalThroughline: Lirien moves from 'controlled precision' (ch1) to 'analytical concern' (target more competent than briefed) to operational decision to delay — emotional state now matches throughline projection.
craftToolCompliance.T21_VulnerabilityLadder: First vulnerability step: Lirien admits internally that Kaelith is 'interesting' beyond target parameters. Does not yet name this as compromise, but recognizes the danger.
craftToolCompliance.T23_MysteryBox: MB-002 opened (Pallor poisoning suggested by accelerated failure), MB-004 advanced (Ythara nature hinted), MB-007 advanced (why Court wants Kaelith dead becomes more mysterious given Warden capacity).
craftToolCompliance.T24_SubtextInDialogue: Kaelith and Lirien's exchange is layered subtext. 'Are you here to kill me?' answered with 'You would not have seen me' — not denial, not confirmation. Both reading each other, neither fully transparent.
craftToolCompliance.T25_ThematicScaffolding: TP-1 (trust as structural failure) — both characters assessing whether the other is threat or resource. Neither trusts, both calculate. Trust will require vulnerability neither has offered.

sceneValueShifts.shift: From 'routine contract execution' to 'target is more dangerous than briefed' — Lirien realizes Kaelith is compressed, not broken, and the file is fundamentally wrong.
From 'professional distance' to 'target has become interesting beyond mission parameters' — direct eye contact, mutual recognition, Lirien holds position instead of withdrawing.
From 'assassination mission' to 'target is anomalous and possibly key to larger crisis' — Seep packet reveals Warden inheritance, execution becomes operationally paradoxical.

emotionalStatesAtBoundaries.chapterStart: Lirien begins in clinical assessment mode, counting patterns, filing observations, experiencing no moral conflict.
emotionalStatesAtBoundaries.chapterEnd: Lirien ends in operational uncertainty — has made the decision to delay execution (violating doctrine), curiosity has compromised professional distance, counting hours until binding pain starts.

planted: Ythara the Unfinished as imprisoned god whose nature dissolves reality (FS-009)
Court contract origin unclear — someone commissioned Kaelith's execution (FS-011 hinted)
48-hour binding deadline — Lirien must act or die (creates urgency for ch3-4)

continuityCitations: Day 1 dawn patrol from ch1 — Pallor tear occurred during Kaelith's seawall walk
Lirien's surveillance from ch1 — direct eye contact moment continues from marketplace scene
Tavik's compromise from ch1 — referenced in Lirien's observation of their interaction
Oathbrand flare from ch1 — now revealed to correlate with Pallor tear, suggesting ontological connection
